mission: Fold installed OpenCode's custom tools and agent improvements into this repo, then checkpoint everything

this_sessions_work.test_status: 21/21 DuckDB tests pass, 0 new typecheck errors in changed files

port_custom_tools.description: Copy 33 custom tools from installed OpenCode's source tree into this repo's packages/opencode/src/tool/. The tools include: propose_plan, revise_plan, comment_plan, review_criticism, send_message, read_messages, prepare_delegation, publish_checkpoint, generate_published_checkpoint_report, generate_report, record_stress_wave, record_execution_wave, prepublication_admitted, prepublication_blocked, prepublication_inconclusive, out_of_scope_finding, inspect_failure, tool_failure, tool_feedback, qa_observed_clean, curate_context, read_artifact, publish_finding, discover_findings, read_source, smart_edit, smart_write, smart_batch, read_lib, prepare_checkpoint, replace_symbol
port_custom_tools.source_hint: Installed OpenCode's packages/opencode/src/tool/ directory (check ~/.opencode/ or the installed binary's package path)

port_tool_registrations.description: Register the 33 tools in packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts. They need entries in the builtin tools list and any layer provisioning they require.

update_agent_profiles.description: The 80 agent .md files in .opencode/agent/ already contain most prompt improvements (anti-stall, transcript handling, etc.). Check if the installed OpenCode has newer versions of these files and sync them.

port_permission_fixes.description: .opencode/opencode.jsonc already has permission fixes. Verify they match the installed version.

port_config_changes.description: Experimental flags (async_task_limit: 999, openTelemetry: true) already in .opencode/opencode.jsonc. Check if any env var exports or Electron process.env injection is needed.

known_blockers: 33 custom tools live in a different copy of the codebase (installed OpenCode). Need to find and extract them.
App-runtime and bootstrap-runtime have pre-existing dirty state from other lanes (DatabaseAdapter Layer.unwrap type issues) — our DuckDB pipeline intentionally avoided touching them.
recommended_order: 1. Checkpoint DuckDB pipeline work (git add + commit)
2. Locate installed OpenCode's tool source files
3. Copy 33 tool files into this repo's packages/opencode/src/tool/

key_context_for_next_session.DuckDB_layer: packages/opencode/src/storage/db.duckdb.ts — reads DuckDBConfig, fires pipeline in background via forkIn(scope)
key_context_for_next_session.DuckDBConfig: packages/opencode/src/storage/duckdb-config.ts — reads OPENCODE_DUCKDB_PATH, defaultLayer uses ConfigService pattern
key_context_for_next_session.Pipeline: packages/opencode/src/storage/pipeline.ts — exports Postgres data to DuckDB via spawn, idempotent via _pipeline_meta, runs in DuckDB.layer init
key_context_for_next_session.Registry: packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts — DuckDBConfig + DuckDB layers provided before Storage.defaultLayer
key_context_for_next_session.AppRuntime: packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts — NOT modified by this session (pre-existing dirty state preserved)
